title: Repair Broken Links In The Z-Axis Cable Carrier
summary: Repair the Z-Axis cable carrier when only a limited number of links are broken by snapping apart the carrier, replacing the broken link sections, verifying divider placement between the cable and air line, and snapping the carrier back together.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the Z-Axis cable carrier has broken links and the damage is limited enough that repair is appropriate instead of full replacement.
not_for: Do not use when damage is beyond a limited number of broken links.
Do not use when divider placement cannot be verified.
Do not use when the air line may contact the FFC after reassembly.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform inspection, snap-apart repair, divider verification, divider positioning, and reassembly of the Z-Axis cable carrier.

safety_notes: This is a physical maintenance repair task involving manual disassembly and reassembly of the cable carrier.
The source packet does not provide explicit LOTO or production-stop requirements for this specific procedure.
access_or_tools_needed: Physical access to the Z-Axis cable carrier

access_required: Physical access to the Z-Axis cable carrier
system_preconditions: Z-Axis cable carrier damage has been identified.
The repair is limited to a limited number of broken links.

visual_references.description: Figure 7-17 Cable Carrier diagram for identifying the Z-Axis cable carrier, broken links, and divider placement context.

steps.title: Put on required PPE
steps.purpose: Prepare safely for physical maintenance work on the cable carrier.
steps.instruction: Put on safety glasses and gloves before starting the repair.
steps.expected_result: Required PPE is in place before handling the cable carrier.
steps.healthy_condition: Safety glasses and gloves are worn.
steps.failure_condition: Required PPE is not worn before beginning the repair.

actions.label: Wear PPE
actions.exact_action: Put on the required PPE: safety glasses and gloves.

steps.title: Gather tools and replacement parts
steps.purpose: Ensure the required tools and replacement carrier parts are available before repair begins.
steps.instruction: Obtain the replacement Z-Axis cable carrier, standard screwdriver, 1.5-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench or socket.
steps.expected_result: All listed tools and parts are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: Replacement carrier and listed hand tools are present.
steps.failure_condition: Required replacement part or tools are missing.

actions.label: Collect tools and parts
actions.exact_action: Obtain the listed tools and parts: replacement Z-Axis cable carrier, standard screwdriver, 1.5-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench/socket.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the cable carrier figure to confirm the component being repaired.

stop_or_escalate_if: Replacement carrier or required tools are unavailable.

steps.title: Inspect broken links and confirm repair scope
steps.purpose: Verify that the cable carrier damage is limited enough for repair rather than full replacement.
steps.instruction: Inspect the Z-Axis cable carrier and identify the broken links to confirm the repair is limited to a limited number of links.
steps.expected_result: Broken links are identified and the repair scope is confirmed as limited.
steps.healthy_condition: Only a limited number of links are broken and the carrier is suitable for repair.
steps.failure_condition: Damage exceeds a limited number of broken links or repair suitability cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Inspect cable carrier damage
actions.exact_action: Inspect the Z-Axis cable carrier and identify the broken links to confirm the repair is limited to a limited number of links.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Identify the Z-Axis cable carrier assembly and the link sections that may be broken.

stop_or_escalate_if: Damage is beyond a limited number of broken links.
It is unclear whether repair is appropriate instead of full replacement.

steps.title: Snap apart the cable carrier
steps.purpose: Open the snap-together carrier so broken link sections can be repaired.
steps.instruction: Snap apart the cable carrier by hand or with the help of a small standard screwdriver.
steps.expected_result: The cable carrier is separated at the required link sections for repair access.
steps.healthy_condition: Carrier links are snapped apart without preventing access to the broken sections.
steps.failure_condition: Carrier cannot be snapped apart as needed or additional damage occurs during separation.

actions.label: Separate carrier links
actions.exact_action: Snap apart the cable carrier by hand or with the help of a small standard screwdriver.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the cable carrier figure to orient to the carrier link construction before snapping links apart.

stop_or_escalate_if: The carrier cannot be snapped apart using the source-supported method.
Additional damage is observed while separating the links.

steps.title: Replace broken link sections
steps.purpose: Restore the damaged portion of the cable carrier using the snap-together link construction.
steps.instruction: Repair the cable carrier by replacing the broken link sections using the snap-together carrier construction.
steps.expected_result: Broken link sections are replaced and the carrier is ready for divider verification and reassembly.
steps.healthy_condition: Damaged link sections have been replaced with intact carrier sections.
steps.failure_condition: Broken sections cannot be replaced or the repaired section does not fit the snap-together construction.

actions.label: Replace broken links
actions.exact_action: Repair the cable carrier by replacing the broken link sections using the snap-together carrier construction.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the figure to identify the cable carrier assembly while replacing broken link sections.

stop_or_escalate_if: Broken sections cannot be repaired with replacement link sections.
Damage appears more extensive than initially assessed.

steps.title: Verify divider presence and position
steps.purpose: Confirm the divider arrangement that separates the cable from the air line within the carrier.
steps.instruction: Check the dividers between the cable and the air line in every second link and verify their position.
steps.expected_result: Divider placement has been checked in every second link where dividers are present.
steps.healthy_condition: Dividers are present between the cable and air line in every second link as described by the source, with placement verified.
steps.failure_condition: Divider placement cannot be verified or the divider arrangement does not protect separation between the air line and FFC.

actions.label: Inspect dividers
actions.exact_action: Check the dividers between the cable and the air line in every second link and verify their position.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for divider locations between the cable and air line in every second link.

stop_or_escalate_if: The air line may contact the FFC.

steps.title: Position dividers against the air line
steps.purpose: Prevent the air line from contacting the FFC after repair.
steps.instruction: Position the dividers all the way to one side against the air line to prevent the air line from contacting the FFC.
steps.expected_result: Dividers are positioned fully to one side against the air line.
steps.healthy_condition: Divider position keeps the air line separated from the FFC.
steps.failure_condition: Divider position does not keep the air line away from the FFC.

actions.label: Set divider position
actions.exact_action: Position the dividers all the way to one side against the air line to prevent the air line from contacting the FFC.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Confirm divider position fully to one side against the air line.

stop_or_escalate_if: Divider placement cannot be set as required.
The air line may still contact the FFC after positioning.

steps.title: Snap the cable carrier back together
steps.purpose: Restore the cable carrier to an assembled condition after link repair and divider verification.
steps.instruction: Reassemble the cable carrier by snapping the links back together.
steps.expected_result: The cable carrier is reassembled with repaired links and verified divider positioning.
steps.healthy_condition: Carrier links are snapped back together and the repaired carrier is intact.
steps.failure_condition: Carrier links do not snap back together correctly or the repaired assembly cannot be restored.

actions.label: Reassemble carrier
actions.exact_action: Reassemble the cable carrier by snapping the links back together.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the figure to confirm overall cable carrier assembly orientation during reassembly.

stop_or_escalate_if: Links do not snap back together.
Divider placement cannot be maintained during reassembly.
The air line may contact the FFC after reassembly.

success_criteria: The Z-Axis cable carrier is repaired.
The carrier links are snapped back together.
Dividers are positioned against the air line.
The air line does not contact the FFC.
healthy_conditions: Only a limited number of links required repair.
Broken link sections were replaced successfully.
Dividers between the cable and air line were checked in every second link.
Dividers are all the way to one side against the air line.
The reassembled carrier is snapped back together.
failure_conditions: Damage is beyond a limited number of broken links.
The carrier cannot be snapped apart or reassembled as required.
The air line may contact the FFC after reassembly.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the damage is beyond a limited number of broken links because the source distinguishes repair from full replacement but does not provide a decision threshold.
Escalate if divider placement cannot be verified.
Escalate if the air line may contact the FFC after reassembly.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall Z-Axis cable carrier assembly, broken link areas, and divider placement context.

missing_details: The source does not provide an exact threshold for what qualifies as a limited number of broken links.
The source does not provide a detailed disassembly sequence beyond snapping the carrier apart.
The source does not provide a detailed reassembly sequence beyond snapping the links back together.
The source does not provide explicit LOTO requirements for this specific procedure in the supplied packet.
The source does not provide an estimated time for this procedure in the supplied packet.
The source packet does not provide exact instructions for use of the 1.5-mm hex wrench or 3/8-in. wrench/socket during this repair.
